title: NOC 65312 – Janitors, caretakers and heavy-duty cleaners | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 65312 professionals are the essential guardians of Canada's commercial and residential infrastructure. Unlike light-duty cleaners, these TEER 5 specialists handle heavy-duty maintenance, groundskeeping, and the operation of industrial cleaning machinery. From maintaining high-rise apartment complexes in Toronto to ensuring the pristine condition of schools and hospitals in Rural Canada, janitors and caretakers provide the foundational safety and hygiene that keeps the country running. In 2026, this role has become a strategic priority for the New TR to PR Pathway, offering a stable bridge to Permanent Residency for those already contributing to the Canadian workforce.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group653 – Support occupations in personal and specialized servicesMinor Group6531 – CleanersNOC Code65312

duties: Operate industrial vacuum cleaners and floor scrubbers to remove heavy debris and maintain large surface areas.
Empty trash cans and other waste containers, ensuring proper sorting of recyclables and hazardous materials.
Wash windows, interior walls, and ceilings using specialized cleaning solutions and equipment.
Clean snow and ice from walkways and parking areas using shovels or small snowblowers.
Cut grass and tend to building grounds, including basic plant care and debris removal.
Clean and disinfect washrooms and fixtures in high-traffic public or industrial settings.
Perform minor repairs and report major building system issues to management.
Move heavy furniture, equipment, and supplies as required for facility setup or maintenance.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required by some institutional employers (e.g., School Boards).
On-the-job training is the primary method of skill acquisition.
WHMIS Certification: Mandatory for handling industrial-strength cleaning chemicals.
Physical stamina and the ability to lift up to 25kg are essential for heavy-duty tasks.
Basic proficiency in English or French is required for safety coordination and following supervisor instructions.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady increase in job openings as Canada continues to invest in "Green Building" maintenance and expanded healthcare facilities. The "Caretaker" aspect of the role is particularly stable, as residential property management companies require year-round staff to manage Canada's growing housing inventory.
labour_market_demand: The demand for janitors and heavy-duty cleaners is Good to Very Good across Canada. In 2026, the market is characterized by a "High Turnover" rate, which creates constant job openings for new immigrants. Demand is exceptionally strong in Manitoba, Yukon, and the Atlantic provinces, where institutional growth (schools and healthcare) has outpaced the local labor supply.
High Accessibility: Short-term work demonstrations often replace the need for formal degrees, allowing for immediate employment upon arrival.
Essential Service Status: Enjoy high job security in a sector that remains operational during all economic cycles.
Groundskeeping Skills: Gain versatile experience in landscaping, snow removal, and basic building system inspections.
Supervisory Potential: Direct progression to Cleaning Supervisor (NOC 62024) or Building Superintendent (NOC 73201).
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time measure targeting 33,000 workers already in Canada. Essential service workers like janitors are expected to be high priority.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for workers with job offers in the Maritimes (NS, NB, PEI, NL).
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Accessible for those working in the maintenance/hospitality sector for 9 consecutive months in BC.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta in eligible service categories.
